chief_complaint: A 60-year-old male patient presents with shortness of breath and wheezing, despite being on dual bronchodilator therapy.
history: The patient has a 10-year history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), with a modified Medical Research Council Dyspnea Scale >/=2 and a COPD Assessment Test (CAT) >/=10. He has been on stable dual bronchodilator therapy for the past year.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, RR 20 bpm, SpO2 92% on room air. Physical examination reveals bilateral wheezing and decreased breath sounds.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: not performed. CAT scores were assessed at baseline, week 6, and week 12.
correct_diagnosis: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) with persistent symptoms
differentials: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) with persistent symptoms
correct_ranking: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) with persistent symptoms
